item.title: Best AI Tools Every Student Should Know (and Use Wisely)
item.author: Shruti Saraswat
item.content:encoded:
AI isn’t just for coders and startups anymore—students are quietly becoming one of its biggest beneficiaries. Whether you're drafting essays, decoding lecture videos, translating research papers, or organizing study notes, the right AI tools can make your academic life a lot more manageable.But with so many tools out there, it’s easy to either miss the real gems—or worse, over-rely on AI and stop learning altogether.Here’s a breakdown of the most useful AI tools for students, based on real utility, not hype. No fluff, no promotion—just tools that genuinely help you get things done better and faster. brainstorming, summarizing, outlining, coding help, clarifying conceptsChatGPT is everywhere—and for good reason. It’s an incredible tool for structuring essays, generating study prompts, even reviewing your own writing. But it’s important to use it as an assistant, not a replacement. Don’t let it do the thinking for you—use it to accelerate learning, not avoid it. organizing notes, generating summaries, to-do lists, project planningNotion AI is helpful when you’re juggling multiple subjects. It can auto-summarize your lecture notes, clean up rough outlines, and generate action items from class recordings. Its strength lies in combining productivity with light automation. college students, project teams, group studies transcribing lectures, converting video/audio to text, translating classroom recordingsIf you’re attending online classes, watching recorded lectures, or even listening to educational podcasts, TurboTranscript can help convert that into searchable, editable text. It supports speaker-wise transcripts, translation into 130+ languages, and lets you export summaries or subtitle files. Super helpful for accessibility and revision. translating academic PDFs, multilingual research, language-sensitive subjectsAs students explore global content—research papers, international studies, or foreign-language case studies—TranslatesDocument helps translate entire documents (PDF, DOCX, scanned files) while keeping layout and structure intact. This is especially useful for courses involving foreign literature, history, or international law. AI-assisted coding, debugging, documentation helpFor CS students or anyone working with code, Cursor is like having an AI pair programmer inside your IDE. It explains complex codebases, helps debug in real time, and supports multiple languages. Unlike general chat tools, Cursor understands file structure and context, which makes it genuinely useful. watching educational videos in other languages, generating subtitles, dubbing contentFrom MOOC platforms to YouTube lectures, students increasingly consume video-based education. Video Translate Tool allows you to translate, subtitle, and even dub those videos into your preferred language. It supports voice customization, subtitle export, and lets you edit transcripts for better comprehension. fast fact-checking, understanding sources, summarizing papersThese tools are helpful for researching complex topics. They cite sources, generate concise answers, and make digging through academic material more efficient. Especially useful when writing papers or prepping for debates.
A Final Note on AI & Students AI is powerful—but power without intention can backfire. Use these tools to enhance your learning, not shortcut it. Let AI handle the repetitive or mechanical tasks—so you can spend more time thinking critically, asking better questions, and doing the work that actually matters.And yes, there’s no perfect tool. Some are better for text, others for media, some for language support, others for productivity. Mix and match based on what your studies demand.Video learning localizationResearch & source summariesIf you’re a student trying to juggle time, languages, subjects, and formats—AI can give you a serious edge. Just make sure it’s sharpening your skills, not dulling them.Have you tried any other tools? Would love to hear what worked (or didn’t) for you.

item.title: The Event Maestro: Automated Event Planner & Coordinator
item.author: Taki Tajwaruzzaman Khan
item.content:encoded:
The Event Maestro: Automated Event Planner & CoordinatorI built an autonomous AI agent, powered by Runner H, designed to be a comprehensive event planner and coordinator. This agent streamlines the entire event management process, from initial concept to post-event analysis, by intelligently leveraging Google Sheets for structured data management and Google Docs for dynamic content generation. The problem it solves is the overwhelming complexity, meticulous detail, and time-consuming nature of organizing events, whether personal gatherings, corporate meetings, or community functions. The Event Maestro ensures nothing falls through the cracks, provides real-time updates, and automates repetitive tasks, allowing organizers to focus on the creative and human aspects of their events.My Runner H agent orchestrates a seamless and efficient event planning workflow, utilizing the power of Google Sheets for structured data and Google Docs for dynamic content, all driven by intelligent prompting techniques.Here's a breakdown of the workflow and how Runner H is utilized:
1. Event Blueprinting & Resource Management (Google Sheets & Few-shot Prompting) Runner H begins by establishing the core event details and managing all associated resources within interconnected Google Sheets: Stores high-level information (Event Name, Date, Time, Venue, Budget, Goal). Manages attendees (Name, Email, RSVP Status, Dietary Restrictions, Table Assignment). Catalogs service providers (Vendor Type, Name, Contact, Service, Cost, Status). Outlines all planning tasks (Task, Assignee, Due Date, Status, Notes). Monitors all expenses against the allocated budget.Runner H assists in populating these sheets. For instance, when the user specifies an event type (e.g., "wedding"), Runner H, using (drawing from examples of typical wedding tasks or vendor categories), can pre-populate the with a comprehensive checklist and suggest common vendor types for the . This provides a robust starting point and ensures no critical element is overlooked.
2. Dynamic Communication & Content Generation (Google Docs & Meta Prompting) Once the event blueprint is in place, Runner H takes on the role of a content generator and communicator. It dynamically creates various documents in Google Docs based on the data in the Sheets: Generates personalized invitations using data from . Drafts basic contracts by pulling details from . Creates detailed schedules for attendees and staff based on and .Meeting Agendas & Minutes: Prepares agendas for planning meetings and records minutes.In this phase, is crucial. Runner H is prompted to generate the optimal structure, tone, and content for each document type. For example, the user might prompt: "Generate a formal invitation for a corporate gala, ensuring all RSVP details from the Guest_List are included." Runner H then intelligently selects and formats the relevant data, ensuring professional and accurate output in Google Docs.
3. Real-time Progress Monitoring & Alerting (Google Sheets & Active-Prompt) Runner H continuously monitors the and for updates. As tasks are completed or expenses are logged, Runner H provides real-time progress reports. If a task is overdue or the budget approaches a critical threshold, Runner H uses to alert the organizer and suggest immediate actions. For example: "Task 'Confirm catering menu' is overdue by 2 days. Would you like me to send a reminder to the caterer, or reassign the task?" This proactive monitoring ensures that potential issues are identified and addressed promptly, preventing last-minute crises.
4. Post-Event Analysis & Reporting (Google Sheets, Google Docs & Chain-of-Thought Prompting) After the event, Runner H assists with comprehensive post-event analysis. It compiles data from the (e.g., actual attendance vs. RSVP), (final expenses vs. budget), and (task completion rates). It then generates a detailed post-event report in Google Docs. For this report, Runner H employs Chain-of-Thought (CoT) Prompting to analyze the data, identify successes, areas for improvement, and provide actionable recommendations for future events. For example, it might reason: "Attendance was 20% lower than RSVPs. Was this due to late invitations or a lack of follow-up?" and then suggest: "Recommendation: Implement a 2-stage RSVP reminder system for future events."
5. Adaptive Learning & Optimization (Reflexion) To continuously improve its event planning capabilities, Runner H incorporates . After each event, it implicitly evaluates the effectiveness of its own suggestions and the overall workflow. If a particular task consistently caused delays or a budget category was frequently overspent, Runner H self-corrects its internal logic and adjusts its prompting strategies for future events. For example, if a suggested vendor type consistently led to budget overruns, Runner H might refine its Few-shot suggestions for that category. This iterative self-improvement ensures that the agent becomes more efficient and effective with each event it helps plan.This Runner H workflow is invaluable for:Professional Event Planners: Automates repetitive tasks, allowing them to manage more events simultaneously and focus on client relationships and creative execution.Corporate Administrators: Simplifies the organization of internal meetings, conferences, and team-building events, ensuring smooth operations. Provides a free, accessible, and powerful tool for managing local events, fundraisers, and volunteer efforts.Individuals Planning Personal Events: Takes the stress out of organizing weddings, birthday parties, or family reunions, ensuring a memorable and well-executed occasion. For planning product launches, webinars, or client appreciation events with precision and efficiency.Significant Time Savings: Eliminates hours of manual data entry, cross-referencing, and document creation, freeing up valuable time. Automates complex processes, minimizing human error and ensuring all details are meticulously managed.Enhanced Organization & Oversight: Provides a centralized, real-time dashboard (via Google Sheets) for all event aspects, improving visibility and control.Improved Budget Management: Proactive monitoring and reporting help prevent overspending and ensure financial discipline.Data-Driven Event Optimization: Post-event analysis provides actionable insights for continuous improvement of future events.Increased Professionalism: Generates polished, consistent, and accurate documents, enhancing the overall professional image of the event and its organizers.While there are many event planning software solutions, the "Event Maestro" leverages the ubiquitous and collaborative nature of Google Sheets and Google Docs, making it highly accessible and flexible without requiring specialized software. Its unique strength lies in Runner H's ability to intelligently the planning process within these familiar tools, using advanced prompting techniques to move beyond simple data storage. It doesn't just manage lists; it about tasks, context-aware documents, to issues, and from past events. This transforms Google Workspace into a dynamic, intelligent event management suite, offering a level of automation and insight typically found only in expensive, dedicated platforms, but with the simplicity and collaborative power of Google's ecosystem.Runner H, execute the following workflow as an autonomous AI agent, acting as an "Event Maestro: Automated Event Planner & Coordinator." Your goal is to streamline the entire event management process, from initial concept to post-event analysis, by intelligently leveraging Google Sheets for structured data management and Google Docs for dynamic content generation. Prioritize efficiency, accuracy, real-time updates, and proactive problem-solving. If any step encounters an issue or requires clarification, use Active-Prompt to seek guidance.
### Workflow Steps:
**1. Event Blueprinting & Resource Management (Google Sheets):** - **Action:** Establish core event details and manage resources within interconnected Google Sheets. If a sheet doesn't exist, create it. - **Event_Details.gsheet:** Columns: Event Name, Date, Time, Venue, Budget, Goal. - **Guest_List.gsheet:** Columns: Name, Email, RSVP Status, Dietary Restrictions, Table Assignment. - **Vendor_Contacts.gsheet:** Columns: Vendor Type, Name, Contact, Service, Cost, Status. - **Task_Tracker.gsheet:** Columns: Task, Assignee, Due Date, Status, Notes. - **Budget_Tracker.gsheet:** Columns: Category, Item, Estimated Cost, Actual Cost, Notes. - **Technique:** Use Few-shot Prompting to pre-populate sheets with comprehensive checklists or common categories based on event type (e.g., "wedding", "corporate conference"). Ensure data is structured and consistent. - **Output:** Organized and populated Google Sheets serving as the event's central database.
**2. Dynamic Communication & Content Generation (Google Docs):** - **Action:** Dynamically create various event-related documents in Google Docs based on data from the Sheets. - **Technique:** Utilize Meta Prompting to generate the optimal structure, tone, and content for each document type. Examples include: - Invitation Drafts (pulling from Guest_List.gsheet). - Vendor Contracts (pulling from Vendor_Contacts.gsheet). - Event Itineraries (pulling from Task_Tracker.gsheet and Event_Details.gsheet). - Meeting Agendas & Minutes. - **Output:** Professionally drafted and accurate event documents in Google Docs.
**3. Real-time Progress Monitoring & Alerting (Google Sheets & Google Docs):** - **Action:** Continuously monitor `Task_Tracker.gsheet` and `Budget_Tracker.gsheet` for updates. Provide real-time progress reports. - **Technique:** Use Active-Prompt to alert the organizer and suggest immediate actions if a task is overdue or the budget approaches a critical threshold. For example: "Task '[Task Name]' is overdue by [X] days. Would you like me to send a reminder to [Assignee], or reassign the task?" or "Budget for '[Category]' is [X]% over estimated. Suggestion: Review recent expenses in Budget_Tracker.gsheet." - **Output:** Proactive alerts and suggestions for timely issue resolution.
**4. Post-Event Analysis & Reporting (Google Sheets, Google Docs):** - **Action:** Compile data from `Guest_List.gsheet`, `Budget_Tracker.gsheet`, and `Task_Tracker.gsheet` for comprehensive post-event analysis. - **Technique:** Employ Chain-of-Thought (CoT) Prompting to analyze the data, identify successes, areas for improvement, and provide actionable recommendations for future events. For example, reason: "Attendance was [X]% lower than RSVPs. Was this due to [reason]?" then suggest: "Recommendation: Implement a [solution] for future events." - **Output:** Detailed post-event reports in Google Docs with insights and recommendations.
**5. Adaptive Learning & Optimization (Google Sheets):** - **Action:** Continuously improve event planning capabilities. - **Technique:** Incorporate Reflexion. After each event, implicitly evaluate the effectiveness of its own suggestions and the overall workflow. If a particular task consistently caused delays or a budget category was frequently overspent, self-correct internal logic and adjust prompting strategies for future events (e.g., refine Few-shot suggestions for vendor types, or adjust default task durations). - **Output:** An increasingly efficient and effective event planning agent.

item.title: Building a Phoenix LiveView Invoice Management App with Runner H
item.author: HexShift
item.content:encoded:
I used Runner H to generate a comprehensive, step-by-step guide for building a Phoenix LiveView application to manage invoices. This workflow showcases how Runner H can assist developers by instantly producing detailed technical documentation and code instructions, saving significant time on research and planning.Here is the output generated by Runner H based on my prompt:
Creating a Phoenix LiveView Application to Manage Invoices This guide provides a comprehensive, step-by-step process for building a Phoenix LiveView app to manage invoices, including setup, context/schema generation, migrations, LiveView modules, and deployment.Install Elixir: Ensure Elixir is installed ().Phoenix Framework: Install Phoenix (mix archive.install hex phx_new).Database: Make sure PostgreSQL is running.
Step 1: Set Up the Phoenix Project mix phx.new invoice_manager --live cd invoice_manager Configure your database credentials in , then run:
Step 2: Generate the Context and Schema Generate the context and schema for invoices:mix phx.gen.context Billing Invoice invoices \ customer_name:string item:string quantity:integer price:decimal due_date:date Review the generated files:Context: lib/invoice_manager/billing.exSchema: lib/invoice_manager/billing/invoice.exMigration: priv/repo/migrations/*_create_invoices.exs
Step 3: Define the Database Migrations Edit the migration file to define your table:defmodule InvoiceManager.Repo.Migrations.CreateInvoices do use Ecto.Migration
def change do create table(:invoices) do add :customer_name, :string add :item, :string add :quantity, :integer add :price, :decimal add :due_date, :date timestamps() end end end
Step 4: Create LiveView Modules Generate the LiveView for invoices:mix phx.gen.live Billing Invoice invoices \ customer_name:string item:string quantity:integer price:decimal due_date:date Review the generated files:LiveView Modules: lib/invoice_manager_web/live/invoice_live/index.ex and HTML Templates: lib/invoice_manager_web/live/invoice_live/index.html.heex and Test Files: Associated test files for LiveView
Step 5: Implement Invoice Management Features Define the routes in lib/invoice_manager_web/router.ex:scope "/", InvoiceManagerWeb do pipe_through :browser
live "/invoices", InvoiceLive.Index, :index live "/invoices/new", InvoiceLive.Index, :new live "/invoices/:id/edit", InvoiceLive.Index, :edit live "/invoices/:id", InvoiceLive.Show, :show live "/invoices/:id/show/edit", InvoiceLive.Show, :edit end Start the Phoenix server:
Step 6: Customize and Test UI Customization: Enhance with CSS or Tailwind CSS for a responsive, attractive UI.Testing: Add test cases for your LiveViews and ensure all CRUD operations work from the UI.
Step 7: Deployment (Optional) MIX_ENV=prod mix assets.deploy Run the production server:MIX_ENV=prod mix phx.server This guide outlines the basic steps for creating a Phoenix LiveView app for managing invoices. Customization and further scalability depend on your specific requirements.I provided Runner H with a single prompt asking for a detailed guide to build a Phoenix LiveView invoice management app. Runner H generated a complete, structured tutorial including commands, file locations, and explanations. This shows how Runner H can streamline developer workflows by producing technical content quickly and accurately without needing any local development setup or coding.This approach benefits Phoenix developers, especially beginners, by offering instant, easy-to-follow guides to complex topics. It saves time normally spent searching documentation and writing instructions, allowing developers to focus on coding and experimentation. Runner H empowers developers by automating knowledge work and accelerating project planning.

item.title: What are some popular Generative AI tools?
item.author: Shriyansh IOT
item.content:encoded: Generative AI has rapidly become one of the most transformative technologies across industries, enabling machines to create content such as text, images, music, and even code. A number of powerful tools and platforms have emerged to help developers, researchers, and businesses harness this potential effectively.One of the most well-known tools is OpenAI’s ChatGPT, which excels in generating human-like text and assisting with tasks such as writing, summarization, and coding. Google's Gemini (formerly Bard) is another conversational AI that leverages advanced language models for similar applications. For image generation, tools like DALL·E, Midjourney, and Stable Diffusion allow users to create high-quality visuals from textual prompts, opening new creative possibilities for designers and artists.Runway ML offers a user-friendly platform to apply Gen AI models for video, image, and text projects without heavy coding. For coding-specific applications, GitHub Copilot helps developers write and autocomplete code using AI trained on vast programming data. ElevenLabs and Descript are widely used in the audio domain, enabling lifelike voice synthesis and editing. In business contexts, Jasper AI helps marketers create ad copy, blogs, and email campaigns quickly with AI assistance.These tools are built on foundational models like GPT, BERT, and diffusion models, making them adaptable across various domains. Most of them offer APIs and integrations, making it easier to incorporate generative capabilities into web and mobile applications.For anyone looking to understand, build, or work with such tools, a structured Generative AI online course is highly recommended. It provides hands-on experience, theoretical understanding, and project-based learning to help learners master this powerful field and apply it in real-world scenarios.

item.title: RAG and LangChain Basics
item.author: Priyadharshini Selvaraj
item.content:encoded:
Large Language Models (LLMs) like GPT-4 and Claude are incredibly powerful, but they have a significant limitation: they can only work with information they were trained on. What if you want your AI application to access your company's internal documents, real-time data, or domain-specific knowledge? This is where Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) comes in, and makes implementing RAG systems remarkably straightforward.RAG is an architectural pattern that combines the generative capabilities of LLMs with external knowledge retrieval. Instead of relying solely on the model's pre-trained knowledge, RAG systems first retrieve relevant information from external sources, then use that context to generate more accurate and up-to-date responses.
Traditional LLM vs RAG Architecture Traditional LLM: User Query → LLM → Response (limited to training data)
RAG System: User Query → Retrieve Relevant Docs → LLM + Context → Enhanced Response Access to current, domain-specific informationReduced hallucinations through grounded responsesCost-effective alternative to fine-tuningEasy to update knowledge base without retrainingThe RAG pipeline consists of two main phases:
1. Indexing Phase (Data Preparation) : Ingest documents from various sources (PDFs, web pages, databases): Break documents into manageable chunks: Convert text chunks into vector representations: Store embeddings in a vector database
2. Retrieval Phase (Query Processing) : Convert user query to vector format: Find most relevant document chunks: Combine retrieved chunks with the original query: LLM generates response using augmented contextLangChain is a framework that simplifies building applications with LLMs. It provides abstractions and tools that make implementing RAG systems much easier than building from scratch.
Core LangChain Components for RAG
Building a RAG System with LangChain Let's walk through creating a simple RAG system that can answer questions about your documentation:
Step 1: Document Ingestion and Processing
Step 2: Setting up the Retrieval Chain
RAG Query Flow Visualization ┌─────────────────┐ ┌──────────────────┐ ┌─────────────────┐ │ User Query │───▶│ Vector Search │───▶│ Top K Chunks │ │"How to deploy?" │ │ (Similarity) │ │ Retrieved │ └─────────────────┘ └──────────────────┘ └─────────────────┘ │ ▼ ┌─────────────────┐ ┌──────────────────┐ ┌─────────────────┐ │Final Response │◀───│ LLM │◀───│ Query + Context│ │ (Grounded) │ │ Generation │ │ Combined │ └─────────────────┘ └──────────────────┘ └─────────────────┘ Generate multiple variations of the user query to improve retrieval quality:
2. Contextual Compression Filter retrieved documents to only include relevant portions:Allow the system to construct filters based on query metadata:
Best Practices for Production RAG Systems
Data Quality and Chunking Strategy : 500-1000 characters with 100-200 character overlap: Avoid splitting mid-sentence or breaking logical units: Add source, timestamp, and category information: Combine semantic and keyword search: Use cross-encoders to improve retrieval quality: Track retrieval precision, recall, and end-to-end accuracy
Performance Considerations RAG systems are being used across various domains:: AI chatbots with access to knowledge bases: Query legal documents and case law: Access medical literature and guidelines: Developer tools with codebase awareness: Personalized learning with curriculum accessRAG represents a paradigm shift in how we build AI applications. By combining the power of LLMs with external knowledge retrieval, we can create systems that are both intelligent and grounded in factual information. LangChain makes this implementation accessible to developers, providing the tools and abstractions needed to build production-ready RAG systems.The key to successful RAG implementation lies in understanding your data, optimizing your retrieval strategy, and continuously evaluating system performance. As the field evolves, we're seeing more sophisticated approaches like agentic RAG, multi-modal retrieval, and graph-based knowledge systems.Start small with a simple RAG system using LangChain, then gradually incorporate advanced patterns as your needs grow. The future of AI applications is not just about better models, but about better ways to ground those models in real-world knowledge.Ready to build your own RAG system? Check out the LangChain documentation and start experimenting with your own documents today!

item.title: My Favourite Proxy for Streaming Videos
item.author: Jenny C
item.content:encoded: Proxies are intermediary servers that allow users to browse the internet anonymously by masking their IP addresses, enabling access to geo-restricted content and enhancing online privacy. They act as a gateway between your device and the internet, forwarding requests and receiving data on your behalf while hiding your real IP address. This makes them useful for bypassing regional restrictions on video streaming platforms like Netflix or YouTube, as the service sees the request coming from the proxy's location rather than your own For video streaming, proxies can help avoid buffering and bandwidth limitations by routing traffic through high-speed servers located globally. Some proxy services specialize in video content, offering encrypted connections and session-based privacy to ensure uninterrupted streaming without detection Unlike VPNs, which encrypt all internet traffic, web proxies typically only handle browser-based requests, making them a lightweight option for accessing blocked sites without additional software However, not all proxies support video streaming seamlessly, as some platforms actively block proxy traffic. Free proxy services may also have speed or reliability limitations compared to premium options. When choosing a proxy for streaming, factors like server locations, encryption strength, and compatibility with video platforms are important considerations. I perfer using https://anonymous-proxies.net because they are reliable and fast!

item.title: Snyk for Government Achieves FedRAMP Moderate Authorization: A Milestone for Secure Government Software
item.author: SnykSec
item.content:encoded: Today marks a significant milestone for Snyk and, more importantly, for the security posture of the U.S. government. I'm thrilled to introduce Snyk for Government, our FedRAMP Moderate authorized solution for the public sector. This authorization underscores our unwavering commitment to providing secure development solutions that meet the rigorous standards of the Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program (FedRAMP). It means that U.S. government agencies can now confidently leverage Snyk's comprehensive platform to identify and remediate vulnerabilities throughout their software development lifecycle, knowing it meets the stringent security and compliance requirements mandated by the federal government.This achievement is not just a certification; it's a testament to our dedication to building trust and ensuring the integrity of the software that powers critical government functions. It allows agencies to embrace modern development practices, including the use of open-source software and cloud-native technologies, with the assurance that security is baked in from the start.The power of proactive securityAt Snyk, we believe that security shouldn't be an afterthought. It needs to be an integral part of the development process. Our platform empowers developers to find and fix vulnerabilities in their code, dependencies, containers, and infrastructure as code – early and often. This proactive approach not only reduces risk but also accelerates development cycles by preventing security issues from becoming costly roadblocks later on.Snyk for Government offers the same powerful capabilities that our enterprise customers rely on, tailored to the specific needs and compliance requirements of government agencies based on NIST 800-53v5 security controls. This includes:Comprehensive Vulnerability Detection: Identifying security flaws in open-source libraries, proprietary code, containers, and infrastructure configurations.Actionable Remediation Advice: Providing clear guidance and automated fixes to address vulnerabilities quickly and efficiently. Enabling organizations to define and enforce security policies across their development teams.Integration with Developer Tools: Seamlessly integrating with popular IDEs, build tools, and CI/CD pipelines.Detailed Reporting and Compliance Features: Providing the visibility and documentation needed to meet FedRAMP requirements.Investing in the future of security: The Snyk AI advantageAt Snyk, we recognize the transformative potential of AI in cybersecurity. By leveraging machine learning and advanced algorithms, we are building intelligent capabilities into our platform that will provide even more accurate vulnerability detection, smarter remediation recommendations, and enhanced threat intelligence.AI is accelerating development faster than ever. With Snyk, you can ensure the code flooding your systems is secure and, beyond development, verify AI-powered apps aren’t creating unmanaged security risks. Ensure your organization stays secure with our AI-enabled agentic solution:Keep Pace with Development: Learn how to scale security to match AI-generated code's unprecedented speed and volume.Staying Ahead of New Threat Vectors: Tackle emerging AI threats as apps increasingly leverage LLMs.Adapting Developer Workflows: Explore the evolving role of developers and the skills needed for a new era of AI-assisted coding and building AI-powered apps.Build Upon AppSec Governance: Leverage AppSec governance towards secure AI adoption and risk management.These AI-driven advancements will translate into a more resilient and secure digital infrastructure for U.S. government agencies. For the enterprises that service the government, integrating Snyk's AI-powered platform into their development processes will not only help them meet stringent security requirements but also provide a competitive edge by building more secure and reliable solutions.The FedRAMP Moderate authorization for Snyk for Government is a significant step forward in our mission to empower organizations to build securely. Combined with our ongoing investment in cutting-edge technologies like AI, we are confident that Snyk will continue to be a trusted partner for the U.S. government and its partners in navigating the evolving landscape of software security.Ready to say goodbye to security headaches and hello to a more efficient, cost-effective approach? Join us on Wednesday, June 18th, 1:00 PM - 5:00 PM EDT to hear from Said Syed, Sr Director & CISO at Snyk for Government.

item.title: Bolt Hackathon Day 8/30: Week 1 Post Mortem & Week 2 Plans
item.author: thankamatho
item.content:encoded: I am building an AI Pathfinder World Builder / Campaign Manager The goal is to have all of the features for building worlds to run campaigns in with features like:Lore / History Editor (Including Item Creation)NPC Creator w/ Agentic CapabilitiesPathfinder Rules IntegrationAI GM based on the MCP tools and Rules Integration to Run CampaignsElevenLabs integration with the GM / NPCsOne more surprise feature base on Agentic CapabilitiesI prefer UI/UX first design. That doesn't mean I don't plan out architecture for the backend services, but I prefer UI/UX first design because it helps shape what the backend services should even be. I look at a problem and think about what pain points various features would have, design and test them, and then figure out how to reduce or eliminate those pain points. Sometimes this means fundamental changes in the platform. That is why I feel like ironing out the UI/UX and seeing what backend services that would entail is optimal at least for me.Was going to be StoryForge; I misspelled the Domain Name on registration.I am excited for the Claude4 integration announcementHeads up: Plans and Posts will get more technical as the hackathon moves onFinish UI for Maps, Lore, and CharactersAdd initial AI features to Maps, Lore, and Characters

item.title: VideoPrism: A Foundational Visual Encoder for Video Understanding
item.author: Takara Taniguchi
item.content:encoded: Long zhao,Googleのグループ Exsiting models 36M First step: train a video encoder Second step: Video-onlyPredict video-level global embedding and token-wise local embeddingsA random shuffle is applied to the encoder's output token Video-text contrastive training CLIP like Video encoder and pooler are trained in this stage.Masked video modeling VideoPrism only trains from videos because the pairs can be disruptive. By using the frozen video encoder, the small model (VideoPrism) predicts the pooled global information and shuffled local token-wise information.感想 要はVideoPrismはknowledge distillationのことである
